pattern: Doing the earlier-available ride first and immediately chaining into the other reduces an order-and-pick decision to checking both category-first plans against every pair.
hints: You must take exactly one land ride and one water ride, and you choose which goes first.
If you finish the first ride at time f, the second ride starts at max(f, its opening time) and ends one duration later.
Try every land/water pair in both orders and keep the smallest overall finish time.

edge_inputs.description: earliest-opening ride is not part of the best plan
many rides each, best uses a mid pair

stress.description: random small ride sets in both categories
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    m = max(1, min(size, 100))
    ls = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    ld = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    ws = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(m)]
    wd = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [ls, ld, ws, wd]
